title: Onboarding audits AGENTS.md outside BEE markers and proposes a minimal header
action: In onboard_bee.mjs computePlan (per D4): when AGENTS.md's content OUTSIDE the BEE:START/END markers contains no non-empty prose line (whitespace and comment-only lines do not count as prose), add plan item {action: 'propose_agents_header', path: 'AGENTS.md'} (deliberate operationalization of D4: any-prose test is the mechanical stand-in for the semantic Q1 check — conservative, never fires on existing prose; lines inside a multi-line HTML comment do not count as prose). Fresh repo plan shape: create_agents_block AND propose_agents_header both present, propose ordered after create; test asserts both items appear. Header goes at the top of the file. on --apply, prepend above the BEE block a minimal header made ONLY of mechanically provable parts: '# <repo folder name>' as title, one loud fill-me gap line for the project one-liner (HTML comment style, e.g. <!-- [unknown] one-line project description - replace me -->), and pointer lines ONLY to files that actually exist at plan time (README.md, docs/specs/system-overview.md, docs/specs/reading-map.md). The item never fires when any prose exists outside the markers - existing user content is never touched, and the newly-created-AGENTS.md case (create_agents_block) also gets the header so fresh repos pass Q1 with pointers from day one. Compose the header BEFORE the mergeAgentsContent call (write it as the existing-content input, then merge-append the block) — do not grow the merge helper. Keep plan/apply semantics and idempotency: second apply is byte-identical, tamper-recovery untouched. Already-onboarded repos (block-only AGENTS.md) flipping from up_to_date to changes_needed on next plan is INTENDED propose-only upgrade behavior — assert it with one test line. Extend test_onboard_bee.mjs: empty repo apply produces the header above the block (title + gap line present); repo whose AGENTS.md already has prose outside markers gets NO propose_agents_header item and its content is preserved byte-for-byte; pointer lines appear only for existing files; re-apply idempotent.

truths: Onboarding a repo with prose outside the BEE markers never adds propose_agents_header and never modifies that prose
The applied header contains the repo folder title, a loud fill-me gap line, and pointers only to files that exist
Second apply after the header is byte-identical (idempotent)

artifacts.substantive: propose_agents_header plan item + header writer, no TODO stubs

key_links: header writer composes content before the existing mergeAgentsContent/extractAgentsBlock path - no second write mechanism, no new merge helper parameter
prohibitions: Never invent prose - the one-liner is always an explicit [unknown] gap, never generated text (per D4 + never-invent)
No change to BEE block content or marker handling
No touching the local COMMAND_KEYS duplicate or its drift test

trace.outcome: computePlan emits propose_agents_header when no prose exists outside the BEE markers (comments/whitespace excluded, multi-line comment aware); apply prepends a minimal header (repo folder title, [unknown] fill-me gap comment, pointers only to existing README.md/docs/specs files) composed before the existing mergeAgentsContent call - one write path, propose-only, existing prose never touched, second apply byte-identical; block-only already-onboarded repos intentionally flip to changes_needed with only the propose item
